items.title: Slot genoot van break en heeft updates over Frimpong en Isak
items.summary: Arne Slot kan tegen Nottingham Forest nog niet beschikken over Jeremie Frimpong en Alexander Isak. Dat maakte de trainer van Liverpool kenbaar op de persconferentie van de club.

items.title: Geschiedenis in voordeel van Man City: Arsenal wacht zenuwslopende eindsprint
items.summary: Resultaten uit het verleden bieden geen garantie voor de toekomst. Dat is voor Arsenal maar goed ook, want de eindsprint van Manchester City bleek de afgelopen jaren telkens van een ander niveau.

items.title: Rondje Europa: wat is Champions League-voetbal toch mooi, sprookjes aanstaande?
items.summary: De heenwedstrijden in de tussenronde van de Champions League stonden garant voor doelpunten. De stunt van de avond kwam op naam van Bodø / Glimt , dat Internazionale versloeg. Het Conference League-bezoek van AZ aan FC Noah staat nog voor de deur. In Rondje Europa blikken we terug én kijken we vooruit.

items.title: New Spurs boss Tudor to play back three vs Arsenal? Plus your views!
items.summary: “We have a lot of injured players, so we need to first find the best system that suits the players that are available at this moment, so a very particular moment.”

items.title: '1-6 is niet zomaar een ongelukkig verlies, het is een pijnlijke vernedering'
items.summary: Newcastle United maakte woensdagavond korte metten met de aspiraties van Qarabag FK om door te stoten naar de achtste finales van de Champions League. De media in Azerbeidzjan schrijven over een beschamende nederlaag.

items.title: How Celtic can overcome Stuttgart in Europa League
items.summary: Former Celtic and Stuttgart defender Andreas Hinkel gives BBC Sport Scotland his analysis of the sides' upcoming Europa League tie.

items.title: Internationale media komen superlatieven tekort voor 'Arctic Magic' van Bodø
items.summary: 'Bodø's Fairytale', 'The King of the North' en 'Arctic Magic'. De internationale media komen superlatieven tekort voor de megastunt van Bodø/Glimt. De Noorse club zorgde woensdag voor een daverende verrassing door Internazionale in de tussenronde van de Champions League op een nederlaag te trakteren.

items.title: Potential game changer? Ferrari's 'clever' upside-down rear wing spotted in testing
items.summary: Technical analyst Sam Collins examines Ferrari's innovative upside-down rear wing during the second day of the final test in Bahrain.

items.title: Champions League talking points: PSG's comeback win, Bodø/Glimt surprise Inter, more
items.summary: We had a very entertaining week of first leg knockout playoff matches in UEFA Champions League! ESPN FC's writers pick out their highs and lows of the tournament to this point.

items.title: Japan replace draws with shootouts and hope to avoid paying World Cup penalty
items.summary: Move is a temporary measure as J.League transitions to European schedule but could benefit national team in US, Canada and Merxico this summer Cynics may say it is no coincidence the J.League has introduced penalty shootouts to replace draws just before the World Cup. Japan have identified the quarter-finals as the target this summer after failing to progress past the last 16 on three of the past four occasions, with two of those disappointments coming after failures from the spot. The 2022 tournament was the worst, with the Samurai Blue, who should have seen off Croatia during normal time, losing the shootout 3-1 in dismal fashion. Continue reading...

items.title: What Guardiola's old newspaper column reveals about Man City's new tactics
items.summary: Pep Guardiola is using new tactics this season. This is why Manchester City's attack is now so narrow.

items.title: Arteta: Arsenal 'didn't show anything' vs. Wolves
items.summary: Arsenal boss Mikel Arteta said his team have only themselves to blame after surrendering a two-goal lead to draw 2-2 with rock-bottom Wolves at Molineux, adding that all criticism of his side was fair after the capitulation.

items.title: Vinícius Jr. racism allegation prompts UEFA probe
items.summary: UEFA will investigate the racism allegations made by Vinícius Júnior in Real Madrid's game against Benfica.

items.title: Gordon makes UCL history with 4 goals in 1st half
items.summary: Newcastle United forward Anthony Gordon has become just the second player to score four goals in the first half of a UEFA Champions League game and the first to do so in the knockout stage.

items.title: Bodo/Glimt add win over Inter to impressive run
items.summary: Champions League runner-up Inter Milan became the latest top team to suffer an upset by Bodø/Glimt in the Champions League, losing 3-1 away in the first leg of their playoff on Wednesday.

items.title: 'Bottle word will be used' as Arsenal blow title advantage
items.summary: After blowing a two-goal lead to draw against the Premier League's bottom club Wolves, there will be no dodging the questions on whether Arsenal are mentally ready to end their 22-year wait to become champions.

items.title: Arteta accepts Arsenal ‘have to blame ourselves’ after blowing lead at Wolves
items.summary: League leaders squander 2-0 lead at bottom side Arteta: ‘We didn’t perform at the level required’ Mikel Arteta admitted Arsenal “have to blame ourselves” after they surrendered a two-goal lead to draw at Wolves. A 94th-minute equaliser cost the Premier League leaders two points as they allowed their rock-bottom opponents back into the game, further calling into question their title credentials. Arsenal are five points clear of Manchester City but have played a game more. The Gunners looked on their way to victory after goals from Bukayo Saka and Piero Hincapié, only to be pegged back by Hugo Bueno and Tom Edozie. Continue reading...

items.title: Gordon channels Shearer after breaking his record
items.summary: Newcastle's Anthony Gordon only had one thought in his mind as his side faced Qarabag in the Champions League...

items.title: Arsenal squander two-goal lead as Wolves score late to earn draw
items.summary: A Ricardo Calafiori own goal in the 94th minute gives Wolves a point. as the Premier League's bottom side come back from two goals down against leaders Arsenal at Molineux.

items.title: Arsenal set up Chelsea showdown in Women’s Champions League with win over Leuven
items.summary: A hard-fought victory over OH Leuven at Meadow Park sent Arsenal through to the quarter-finals of the Champions League, where they will face Chelsea next month. The visitors tested Renée Slegers’s side when Sára Pusztai cancelled out Alessia Russo’s goal but a penalty from Mariona Caldentey and second from Russo secured the win, earning them a comfortable 7-1 aggregate score. This was a disjointed performance from the hosts but it will have done little to dampen the high spirits in north London. Continue reading...
